mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which systematically reduces corporate earnings expectations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in greater than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains elevated for more than two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of accelerating retail sales and decelerating inflation indicates robust consumer demand without price pressures, expanding corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing, or positive returns fail to materialize when CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes exceed 0.8%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate supply-side capacity utilization and near-term earnings momentum, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns within a single trading week.
failure_condition: Direction flips to negative during monetary tightening cycles despite INDPRO acceleration, or 5-day forward returns show zero correlation with industrial production growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion and improved economic fundamentals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon regardless of short-term market noise.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes (p-value > 0.10), or the signal consistently underperforms during stagflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 260.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated central bank tightening and higher real discount rates, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 260.0, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns when inflation is supply-driven rather than demand-driven.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm genuine labor market tightening and rising wage growth, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when the unemployment rate falls below 3.5% due to labor shortage constraints, or interaction effects are statistically indistinguishable from independent single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing below 1.4 million units signals a contraction in the housing sector and reduced construction activity, which drags on broader economic growth and yields negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly returns persist in greater than 60% of instances where HOUST remains below 1400.0, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of government housing stimulus.
